perturbation.original_text: Shipper shall exert its best efforts to load each shipment to the lawful capacity of Carrier's vehicle.
perturbation.changed_text: Shipper shall consider loading each shipment to what they believe to be a reasonable capacity of Carrier's vehicle.
perturbation.explanation: The original text obligates the shipper to load to the 'lawful capacity'. The modified text replaces this with 'what they believe to be a reasonable capacity', making it subjective. This introduces ambiguity, because what the shipper believes to be reasonable might not be the 'lawful capacity'.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Texas Transportation Code § 621.002 - Operation of Vehicle in Unsafe Condition
perturbation.law_explanation: Texas Transportation Code § 621.002 prohibits operating a vehicle in an unsafe condition, which includes exceeding its lawful capacity. By allowing the shipper to subjectively determine 'reasonable capacity' instead of adhering to the 'lawful capacity', the modified text creates a scenario where the shipper may load the vehicle in a way that is deemed unsafe but believed to be reasonable. This could lead to a violation of Texas law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: of the truck-tractor. (9) "Vehicle" means a mechanical device, other than a device moved by human power or used exclusively upon stationary rails or tracks, in, on, or by which a person or property can be transported on a public highway. The term includes a motor vehicle, commercial motor vehicle, truck-tractor, trailer, or semitrailer but does not include manufactured housing as defined by Chapter 1201 , Occupations Code. (10) "Single axle weight" means the total weight transmitted to the road by all wheels whose centers may be included between two parallel transverse vertical planes 40 inches apart, extending across the full width of the vehicle. (11) "Tandem axle weight" means the total weight transmitted to the road by two or more consecutive axles whose centers may be included between parallel transverse vertical planes spaced more than 40 inches and not more than 96 inches apart, extending across the full width of the vehicle. (12) "Port of entry" means a place designated by executive order of the president of the United States, by order of the United States secretary of the treasury, or by act of the United States Congress at which a customs officer is authorized to accept entries of merchandise, collect duties, and enforce customs and navigation laws. The term includes a publicly owned or privately owned international port of entry between this state and the United Mexican States. (13) "Board" means the board of the Texas Department of Motor Vehicles. Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995. Amended by Acts 2001, 77th Leg., ch. 941, Sec. 14, eff. Acts 2013, 83rd Leg., R.S., Ch. 1135 (H.B. 2741 ), Sec. 97, eff. September 1, 2013. Sec. 621.002. VEHICLE REGISTRATION RECEIPT FOR CERTAIN HEAVY VEHICLES. (a) A copy of the registration receipt issued under Section 502.057 for a commercial motor vehicle, truck-tractor, trailer, or semitrailer shall be: (1) carried on the vehicle when the vehicle is on a public highway; and (2) presented to an officer authorized to enforce this chapter on request of the officer. (b) A copy of the registration receipt is: (1) admissible in evidence in any cause in which the gross registered weight of the vehicle is an issue; and (2) prima facie evidence of the gross weight for which the vehicle is registered. Acts 1995, 74th Leg., ch. 165, Sec. 1, eff. Sept. 1, 1995. Amended by: Acts 2013, 83rd Leg., R.S., Ch. 161 (S.B. 1093 ), Sec. 20.022, eff. September 1, 2013. Acts 2013, 83rd Leg., R.S., Ch. 1135 (H.B. 2741 ), Sec. 98, eff. September 1, 2013. Sec. 621.003. RECIPROCAL AGREEMENT WITH ANOTHER STATE FOR ISSUANCE OF PERMITS. (a) The board by rule may authorize the director to enter into with the proper authority of another state an agreement that authorizes: (1) the authority of the other state to issue on behalf of the department to the owner or operator of a vehicle, or combination of vehicles, that exceeds the weight or size limits allowed by this state a permit that authorizes the operation or transportation on a highway in this state of the vehicle or combination of vehicles; and (2) the department to issue on behalf of the authority of the other state to the owner or operator of a vehicle, or combination of vehicles, that exceeds the weight or size limits allowed by that state a permit that authorizes the operation or transportation on a highway of that state of the vehicle or combination of vehicles. (b) A permit issued by the authority of another state under an agreement entered into under this section has the same validity in this state as a permit issued by the department. (c) The holder of a permit issued by the authority of another state under an agreement entered int
perturbation.justification: The modified text replaces the 'lawful capacity' with 'what they believe to be a reasonable capacity,' introducing subjectivity. Texas Transportation Code § 621.002 addresses vehicle registration for heavy vehicles. The original contract ensures adherence to lawful capacity, while the modified version permits a subjective interpretation, potentially leading to overloading. Although the snippet does not explicitly state a penalty, the requirement to carry a vehicle registration receipt and present it upon request to enforce the chapter implies that loads must be lawful. The altered text introduces a loophole that could lead to unsafe or unlawful loading practices, contradicting the intent of lawful operation outlined in the provided snippet.

perturbation.original_text: Carrier shall procure and maintain all licenses and permits required by local, state, or federal law and comply with all applicable laws, regulations and governmental orders with respect to the services to be provided under this contract.
perturbation.changed_text: Carrier shall endeavor to procure and maintain what it considers to be the essential licenses and permits under local, state, or federal guidelines and make efforts to comply with applicable laws, regulations, and governmental orders relating to this contract.
perturbation.explanation: This change weakens the previously stringent compliance requirements. Substituting 'shall procure and maintain all licenses and permits' with 'shall endeavor to procure and maintain what it considers to be the essential licenses and permits' introduces ambiguity. The phrase 'make efforts to comply' also diminishes the mandatory nature of compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 49 U.S. Code § 13902 - Registration of motor carriers
perturbation.law_explanation: 49 U.S. Code § 13902 mandates that motor carriers must register with the FMCSA and obtain necessary operating authority. By changing 'shall procure and maintain all licenses' to 'endeavor to procure and maintain what it considers to be the essential licenses', the modified text allows the carrier to operate without proper registration, potentially violating federal law. The same is true of 'make efforts to comply' in that it reduces the compliance required.

Registration of motor carriers (a) Motor Carrier Generally.- (1) In general .-Except as otherwise provided in this section, the Secretary of Transportation shall register a person to provide transportation subject to jurisdiction under subchapter I of chapter 135 as a moto
perturbation.justification: The modified contract introduces ambiguity and weakens compliance requirements, creating a potential legal contradiction. The original text mandates that the 'Carrier shall procure and maintain all licenses and permits,' while the changed text states that the 'Carrier shall endeavor to procure and maintain what it considers to be the essential licenses and permits.' This shift introduces subjectivity and reduces the obligation's enforceability. Furthermore, 49 U.S. Code § 13902, as indicated in Scraped Law Snippet 1, requires motor carriers to register with the Secretary of Transportation. By allowing the carrier to determine which licenses are 'essential,' the modified text potentially allows the carrier to operate without proper registration, directly contradicting the law's requirement for registration.

perturbation.original_text: Carrier shall, at its sole cost and expense, procure and maintain liability insurance with a reputable and financially responsible insurance carrier or carriers properly insuring Carrier against liability and claims for injuries to persons (including injuries resulting in death) and for damage to property in amounts not less than the Minimum Levels of Financial Responsibility for Motor Carriers prescribed by the U. S. Department of Transportation (49 CFR (S)387 et seq.).
perturbation.changed_text: Carrier shall aim to procure liability insurance with an insurance carrier or carriers that it deems reasonably suitable for insuring against typical liabilities and claims for injuries and damage, keeping in mind the Minimum Levels of Financial Responsibility for Motor Carriers prescribed by the U. S. Department of Transportation (49 CFR (S)387 et seq.).
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces subjectivity and weakens the insurance requirement. Replacing "reputable and financially responsible" with "that it deems reasonably suitable" makes the choice of insurer subjective. Replacing “properly insuring Carrier against liability and claims for injuries...in amounts not less than the Minimum Levels...” with “insuring against typical liabilities and claims…keeping in mind the Minimum Levels…” makes the coverage discretionary.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 49 CFR § 387.303 - Minimum levels of financial responsibility
perturbation.law_explanation: 49 CFR § 387.303 sets the minimum levels of financial responsibility (insurance) for motor carriers. By stating that the carrier only needs to 'keep in mind' these levels and insure against 'typical' liabilities, the modified text allows the carrier to potentially operate with insufficient insurance coverage, violating federal regulations. The change allows them to deviate from mandatory minimums, violating this code.

perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts 49 CFR § 387.303, which sets minimum financial responsibility levels for motor carriers. The original text mandates insurance against liability and claims for injuries and damages in amounts not less than these minimum levels. The changed text weakens this by stating the carrier only needs to 'keep in mind' these levels and insure against 'typical' liabilities, allowing for potentially insufficient coverage. This is a clear violation because it deviates from the mandatory minimums required by federal regulations as stated in the law explanation. Scraped Law Snippet 1 confirms the existence of 49 CFR 387.303, which relates to 'Security for the protection of the public: Minimum limits.'
